rulings.Case Title: ELEN MEDER VS. HAVERST SMALL BUSINESS FINANCE, LLC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Second Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enlarge Time For Filing Demurrer And Motion To Strike
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 8, 2024 line . DEFENDANT HARVEST SMALL BUSINESS FINANCE, LLC SECOND AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENLARGE TIME FOR FILING DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE is moot. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ELEN MEDER VS. HAVERST SMALL BUSINESS FINANCE, LLC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Second Amended Notice Of Motin An Dmotio To Vacate The Pending Default Based On Inadvertence
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 8, 2024 line . DEFENDANT HARVEST SMALL BUSINESS FINANCE, LLC SECOND AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTIN AN DMOTIO TO VACATE THE PENDING DEFAULT BASED ON INADVERTENCE is GRANTED. Defendants shall file a response to the operative complaint by 2:00 p.m. on February 8, 2024. Plaintiff to provide the Court and Defense counsel with a list of costs (e-filing fees, copies, courtesy copy delivery, parking, etc) with evidence associated with obtaining a default, setting a prove-up hearing and filing an opposition to this motion by 9:00 a.m. on February 8, 2024. Defendant to prepare a proposed order granting this motion including a line awarding Plaintiff her costs, leaving the amount blank. A hard copy of the order must be presented to the Court for execution at the hearing. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: EQUITY RESIDENTIAL MANAGEMENT, LLC VS. KIRSTIN OAKES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion For Entry Of Judgment For Possession And Monetary Damages Pursuant To Stipulation
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 8, 2024 line . PLAINTIFF EQUITY RESIDENTIAL MANAGEMENT, LLC PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT FOR POSSESSION AND MONETARY DAMAGES PURSUANT TO STIPULATION is DENIED. There is an active judgment in this matter already. Neither party sought to vacate that judgment. Plaintiff is now asking for an entry of a second judgment in the same case without any authority for such relief. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ALL HALLOWS PRESERVATION, LP VS. GARY TAYLOR ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 8, 2024 line . DEFENDANT GARY TAYLOR DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions supporting each element of the cause of action for forcible detainer. Plaintiff must also allege facts, not conclusions, to support malice. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
